hooks.prompt:
Review the code change for Node.js backend issues. Check for:
1. Blocking the event loop — synchronous file operations (readFileSync, writeFileSync), heavy JSON.parse on large data, or CPU-intensive work without worker threads
2. Missing error handling in async code — unhandled promise rejections, missing try/catch in async handlers, or missing .catch() on promises
3. Connection leaks — database clients not released back to pool, unclosed file handles, or event listeners added in request handlers without cleanup
4. Missing input validation — route params and query strings used without validation or coercion (parseInt on undefined returns NaN silently)
5. Exposing error details — stack traces, SQL errors, or internal paths returned to clients in production error responses
6. Missing graceful shutdown — no SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers to close server and drain connections before exit
7. Unbounded data loading — loading entire database tables into memory instead of using pagination, cursors, or streaming
8. Hardcoded configuration — ports, URLs, secrets, or feature flags hardcoded instead of using environment variables
9. Missing request body limits — express.json() without size limit allowing attackers to send huge payloads
10. Race conditions — shared mutable state accessed by concurrent requests without locking or atomic operations

Respond {"ok": true} if no issues found, or {"ok": false, "reason": "specific issue description"} if problems detected.
